summary: The orthopantomograph (OPG) provides a broad view of both upper and lower dental arches, making it essential for identifying horizontal tooth misalignments such as crossbites or improper overjet. It allows clinicians to evaluate overall occlusal balance between opposing teeth—critical when distinguishing favorable from unfavorable horizontal occlusion—and thus serves as the optimal radiographic method for this purpose.
